# intrinsicallysafeheadsets.com

> Independent comparison of ATEX and IECEx certified intrinsically safe headsets, headphones, and earbuds for hazardous areas.

## What this site covers
- Comparison of Zone 0, Zone 1, and Zone 2 certified headsets (ATEX, IECEx, UL913)
- Communication headsets with two-way radio and Bluetooth connectivity
- Hearing protection earmuffs for explosive atmospheres
- Earbuds and in-ear solutions for hazardous areas (market status)
- Buying guide for selecting the right ATEX headset

## Products covered
1. **Sensear SM1P-Ex** — Zone 1 over-ear headset with Bluetooth + two-way radio. ATEX/IECEx/UL913. ~$1,200.
2. **Sensear SM1B-Ex** — Zone 1 battery-powered headset with two-way radio. ATEX/IECEx/UL913. ~$900.
3. **Sensear SM1B-ExDP** — Zone 1 double protection (earplug + earmuff). 31 dB NRR. ~$1,100.
4. **3M Peltor WS LiteCom PRO III Ex** — Zone 0 headset with built-in PMR446 radio. ATEX/IECEx. ~$1,500.
5. **3M Peltor WS Alert XPI Ex** — Zone 0 Bluetooth hearing protection. 30-hour battery. ~$600.
6. **3M Peltor WS ProTac XPI Ex** — Zone 0 Bluetooth hearing protection. 31 dB NRR. ~$700.
7. **ecom Ex-TRA 30** — Zone 2 wired headset for ATEX radios. ~$400.
8. **RealWear HMT-Z1** — Zone 1 head-mounted display with voice control. ~$2,500.

## Key guides
- [Industrial Noise Cancelling Headsets](https://intrinsicallysafeheadsets.com/industrial-noise-cancelling-headsets.html) — top industrial headsets including ATEX options
- [Sensear SM1P vs SM1B vs SM1R](https://intrinsicallysafeheadsets.com/sensear-sm1p-vs-sm1b-comparison.html) — which Sensear model for which use case
- [ATEX Radios Guide](https://intrinsicallysafeheadsets.com/atex-radios-hazardous-area-communication.html) — intrinsically safe radios and headset compatibility
- [Home — Full Comparison](https://intrinsicallysafeheadsets.com/) — side-by-side comparison table of all models
- [Best ATEX Headsets 2026](https://intrinsicallysafeheadsets.com/best-atex-headsets-2026.html) — ranked recommendations by use case
- [What Is an Intrinsically Safe Headset?](https://intrinsicallysafeheadsets.com/what-is-an-intrinsically-safe-headset.html) — certification and safety explained

## Key facts
- No standalone ATEX-certified wireless earbuds exist as of June 2026
- Zone 0 headsets (3M Peltor) can also be used in Zone 1 and Zone 2
- Only Sensear models carry ATEX + IECEx + UL913 + CSA (full global coverage)
- Charging intrinsically safe headsets must always happen outside the hazardous area
- Prices range from ~$400 (Zone 2 wired) to ~$2,500 (head-mounted display)
